perturbation.original_text: Prior to providing the Confidential Information to any employee or consultant, the receiving Party shall ensure that an executed nondisclosure agreement is in place with each employee or consultant with protections that are substantially similar to the protections in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving Party is encouraged to have nondisclosure agreements with employees and consultants who have access to Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall ensure' to 'is encouraged to' weakens the mandatory requirement for non-disclosure agreements. This introduces a potential contradiction: while the agreement aims to protect Confidential Information, this change makes it optional to have NDAs in place with those handling it. This weakening could contradict the overall intent of the NDA. If the receiving party chooses not to have NDAs, it weakens the ability to seek remedy in case of breach of this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing Party. The disclosu re of Confidential Information by the disclosing Party shall not constitute an express or implied grant to the receiving Party, of any rights to or under the disclosing Party’s patents, copyrights, trade secrets, trademarks or any other intellectual proper ty rights. Upon the disclosing Party’s request, at the completion of the Purpose, all Confidential Information shall be either returned to the disclosing Party or destroyed and certified in writing that such Confidential Information has been destroyed. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, the receiving Party will continue to be bound by its confidentiality and other obligations hereunder in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated NDAs for employees/consultants accessing confidential information ('shall ensure'). The changed text weakens this to an encouragement ('is encouraged to'). The contradicted text states that the receiving party will continue to be bound by its confidentiality and other obligations hereunder in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Because the change makes NDAs optional, it weakens the enforceability of confidentiality and contradicts the intent that the receiving party will continue to be bound by confidentiality, creating a conflict as described by the perturbation explanation provided.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the disclosing Party’s request, at the completion of the Purpose, all Confidential Information shall be either returned to the disclosing Party or destroyed and certified in writing that such Confidential Information has been destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon request, Confidential Information should be handled appropriately.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specifics of 'returned' or 'destroyed and certified', the contract creates ambiguity about how Confidential Information is handled after the project. While it says the info 'should be handled appropriately,' it does not define that. This contrasts with the earlier stated goal of protecting Confidential Information, as it leaves the final disposition undefined and unenforceable. If the final disposition is undefined and unenforable, there is no way to enforce the protection of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall continue for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date or until such time as a definitive a greement(s) is entered into by the Parties with respect to the Purpose provided however that the obligations contained in this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the date of termination of this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a clear procedure for handling confidential information upon request: either return or destroy and certify. The changed text replaces this with a vague requirement to handle the information 'appropriately,' creating ambiguity. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the confidentiality obligations survive the termination of the agreement for five years. Because the specifics for the protection of Confidential Information are not defined, and made vague in the changed text, this contradicts the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the sole and entire agreement between the Parties related to the disclosure of Confidential Information with respect to the Purpose. This Agreement may only be modified in a writing executed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement outlines the general understanding between the Parties regarding Confidential Information. Modifications can be discussed and implemented as needed.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'sole and entire agreement' with 'general understanding,' and 'may only be modified in writing' with 'modifications can be discussed and implemented as needed,' the contract now suggests that there might be other agreements or understandings beyond this document and that the agreement can be modified informally. This undermines the enforceability of the agreement and creates a contradiction with the initial intent of having a complete and binding agreement. The modification clause creates uncertainty because 'implemented as needed' does not ensure both parties agreement. The lack of requirement of both parties agreement contradics the whole nature of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Other than this Agreement, no contract or agreement providing for any relationshi p involving the Parties (whether relating to the Purpose or otherwise) will be deemed to exist between the Parties unless and until a final definitive agreement(s) has been executed and delivered by each of the Parties, and unless and until any such defini tive agreement(s) has been executed an delivered, neither Party will be under any legal obligation of any kind or nature whatsoever, except for the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement
perturbation.justification: The original text established the agreement as the 'sole and entire agreement' and stipulated that modifications must be in writing. The changed text weakens this by describing it as a 'general understanding' that 'can be discussed and implemented as needed,' thus opening the door to informal modifications. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which explicitly states that no other agreements exist unless formalized in writing and executed by both parties. This change introduces ambiguity and directly conflicts with the requirement of a final, definitive, and executed agreement as the only binding obligation.
